expected_professional_report_description: An active water leak was observed beneath the kitchen sink. The cabinet base showed evidence of moisture, and a drip was noted at the P-trap connection. Immediate repair is recommended to prevent further water damage to the cabinet structure and subfloor.
expected_plain_english_summary: There is an active leak under the kitchen sink at the P-trap. The cabinet floor is already wet. A plumber should fix this soon to prevent water damage from spreading.
expected_recommended_action: Have a licensed plumber inspect and repair the P-trap connection. After the repair, check the cabinet base and subfloor for any existing water damage.

expected_professional_report_description: Damaged and deteriorated boards were observed on the rear exterior wall near the window. The compromised material may allow moisture intrusion and should be repaired to maintain the integrity of the building envelope.
expected_plain_english_summary: Some boards on the back exterior wall near the window are damaged. If left unrepaired, water could get into the wall and cause bigger problems over time.
expected_recommended_action: Have a handyman or contractor replace the damaged exterior boards. Inspect for any underlying moisture damage and ensure proper caulking and sealing around the window after the repair.

expected_professional_report_description: A missing outlet cover plate was observed in the garage with exposed wiring visible at the receptacle. This condition presents a potential shock and fire hazard and requires immediate correction.
expected_plain_english_summary: An outlet in the garage is missing its cover plate and has exposed wiring. This is a safety hazard that could cause a shock. It is an inexpensive and easy fix.
expected_recommended_action: Install an appropriate cover plate on the garage outlet. Before covering, verify the outlet and wiring are in good condition.

expected_title: Missing Shingles on Rear Roof Slope
expected_professional_report_description: Multiple missing shingles were observed on the rear slope of the roof, visible from ground level. The exposed underlayment is susceptible to moisture intrusion and should be repaired promptly to prevent water damage to the roof decking and interior.
expected_plain_english_summary: Several shingles are missing on the back of your roof. This can allow water to get underneath and cause damage over time if not repaired soon.
expected_recommended_action: Have a licensed roofer replace the missing shingles. After the repair, inspect the attic for any signs of existing moisture intrusion or damage to the roof decking.

expected_title: Clogged Condensate Drain Line at Air Handler
expected_professional_report_description: The condensate drain line at the air handler was observed to be clogged, with standing water present in the drip pan. If left unaddressed, overflow could result in water damage to surrounding materials and potential mold growth.
expected_plain_english_summary: The drain line on your air conditioner is clogged and water is collecting in the overflow pan. A technician should clear the line before it overflows and causes water damage.
expected_recommended_action: Have an HVAC technician clear the condensate drain line. After the repair, inspect the area around the air handler for any existing water damage to adjacent materials.

expected_title: Water Stain on Living Room Ceiling
expected_professional_report_description: A water stain was observed on the living room ceiling directly below the second floor bathroom. The stain appeared dry at the time of inspection, indicating a prior or intermittent moisture source. The origin of the leak should be identified before repairs are made.
expected_plain_english_summary: There is a water stain on your living room ceiling below the upstairs bathroom. The stain is dry now but something leaked in the past. The source needs to be found before patching the ceiling.
expected_recommended_action: Have a plumber inspect the bathroom above for leaking fixtures or supply lines. Once the source is confirmed and repaired, have the ceiling drywall patched and repainted.

expected_title: Double-Tapped Breaker in Main Electrical Panel
expected_professional_report_description: A double-tapped breaker was observed in the main electrical panel, with two circuit wires connected to a single breaker terminal. This condition is a potential fire hazard and does not comply with standard electrical installation practice.
expected_plain_english_summary: Two wires are connected to one breaker in your electrical panel, which is not allowed and can be a fire hazard. An electrician needs to correct this.
expected_recommended_action: Have a licensed electrician correct the double-tapped breaker by installing a tandem breaker or adding a subpanel as appropriate. Verify the panel is otherwise in compliance after the repair.

expected_title: Horizontal Crack in Poured Concrete Foundation Wall
expected_professional_report_description: A horizontal crack was observed in the poured concrete foundation wall, extending approximately 8 feet along the mid-section of the wall. Horizontal cracks in foundation walls can indicate lateral soil pressure and potential structural compromise, and require evaluation by a structural engineer.
expected_plain_english_summary: There is a long horizontal crack in your basement foundation wall. This type of crack can be a sign of serious structural stress and needs to be evaluated by a structural engineer before it gets worse.
expected_recommended_action: Engage a licensed structural engineer to evaluate the crack and determine the appropriate repair scope. Do not delay, as horizontal foundation cracks can indicate active wall movement.

expected_title: Standing Water in Dishwasher After Cycle
expected_professional_report_description: Standing water was observed in the bottom of the dishwasher following the completion of a full wash cycle, indicating a drain failure. The cause may be a clogged filter, obstructed drain hose, or a faulty drain pump.
expected_plain_english_summary: Your dishwasher is not draining and is leaving standing water after each wash. An appliance technician can find and fix the cause.
expected_recommended_action: Check the dishwasher drain filter for debris and clear if clogged. If the problem persists, have an appliance technician inspect the drain hose and pump.

expected_professional_report_description: The soil grading at the rear of the home was observed to slope toward the foundation, which may direct surface water toward the crawl space. Proper grading should slope away from the foundation at a minimum of 6 inches over the first 10 feet.
expected_plain_english_summary: The ground at the back of your home slopes toward the house instead of away from it. This means rainwater runs toward your foundation, which can cause water to get into the crawl space over time.
expected_recommended_action: Have a contractor regrade the soil at the rear of the home to direct surface water away from the foundation. After regrading, inspect the crawl space for any existing moisture intrusion.

expected_professional_report_description: The pull-down attic stair hatch was observed to have no insulation cover, creating a thermal bypass in the ceiling assembly. This condition reduces the overall energy efficiency of the home by allowing conditioned air to escape into the attic.
expected_plain_english_summary: The attic access hatch has no insulation on it, which means heated or cooled air is escaping into the attic. An insulated cover is an easy and inexpensive fix.
expected_recommended_action: Install a pre-made attic stair insulation cover or construct one from rigid foam board. This is a straightforward DIY project that can significantly improve energy efficiency.

expected_title: Garage Door Auto-Reverse Safety Feature Not Functioning
expected_professional_report_description: The garage door opener was tested and found not to auto-reverse when an obstruction was placed in the path of the closing door. The absence of a functioning safety reversal feature presents a risk of injury or entrapment.
expected_plain_english_summary: Your garage door does not stop and reverse when something is in its path. This is a safety hazard that could cause injury, especially to children or pets, and should be repaired promptly.
expected_recommended_action: Have a garage door technician adjust or repair the auto-reverse mechanism. If adjustment does not resolve the issue, replace the garage door opener with a current code-compliant model.

expected_title: Mold Growth on Basement Walls and Floor Joists
expected_professional_report_description: Visible mold growth was observed on the basement walls and the underside of the floor joists. A musty odor was present throughout the basement at the time of inspection, indicating an ongoing or significant moisture condition that requires professional remediation.
expected_plain_english_summary: There is visible mold growing on the basement walls and the wooden beams above, along with a strong musty smell. Mold is a health hazard and will spread unless the moisture source is fixed and the mold is professionally removed.
expected_recommended_action: Engage a certified mold remediation professional to assess the extent of the mold and perform remediation. Identify and eliminate the moisture source prior to or concurrent with remediation to prevent recurrence.

expected_title: Termite Mud Tubes on Foundation Wall in Crawl Space
expected_professional_report_description: Mud tubes consistent with subterranean termite activity were observed on the foundation wall in the crawl space. Active or prior termite infestation should be evaluated by a licensed pest control professional, and any structural wood damage should be assessed separately.
expected_plain_english_summary: There are signs of termite activity in the crawl space. Termites can cause serious structural damage over time and should be treated by a pest control professional as soon as possible.
expected_recommended_action: Engage a licensed pest control professional to inspect for active termite activity and recommend a treatment plan. Following treatment, have a contractor assess the structural wood members in the crawl space for any existing damage.
